zh.lifeSummary: Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn於1552年出生在英國蘇格蘭素斯 藍納可爾藍納可。他於大約1576年與Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是9個兒子的父母。他去世於1623年8月15日在他的家鄉享年71歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾可萊。

nameMeanings.name: Lockhart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Old French locart, a Normanno-Picard form of Old French lo(s)chart ‘one who squints, who is cross-eyed’, attested in 13th- and 14th-century Arras (Pas-de-Calais) as Louchard, Lochard, Locard and Locart. It is a derivative of Latin luscus ‘one-eyed’. In France Locard is mostly found in Normandy (especially Calvados) c. 1900, while Louchart is mainly concentrated in Pas-de-Calais at the same time.
Americanized form of German   Luckhardt  .

History: According to family tradition, Sir Simon Locard held the key to the locked silver casket in which Robert the Bruce's heart was taken to be buried in the Holy Land in 1329. This unverifiable story is no doubt related to the later spelling of the Scottish surname as Lockhart and to the addition of a heart and fetterlock to the family coat of arms (date unknown).

variants.name: Lockard
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart   1 and 2.

variants.name: Lockart
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockheart
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .

variants.name: Lockert
meanings: German: variant of   Lockhart   3.

variants.name: Luckhardt
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic female personal name Liutgard, a compound of liud ‘people, tribe’ + gard ‘protective enclosure, yard’. Liutgard was a popular name in medieval times; among others it was the name of the daughter of emperor Otto I and the wife of Charlemagne.

variants.name: Lochard
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lockhart  .
French: from a derivative of Old French loche ‘loach’, later also ‘species of giant slug’, hence, in the latter case, a nickname for a slow man. This surname is also found in Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: VI
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 韋, see   Wei   3.

variants.name: Vy
meanings: Vietnamese: variant of   Vi  .
Cambodian: written វី, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning ‘to spread out’.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Ceghorn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Central and southeastScotland, North eastern England): habitational name from a place in Lanarkshire called Cleghorn.

variants.name: Claghorn
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cleghorn  .
History: James Claghorn was shipped to New England in 1651 as a prisoner,having been one of the Scottish opponents of Oliver Cromwell. In 1662he is recorded as a freeman of Barnstable, MA.

variants.name: Gleghorn
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Cleghorn  .

nameMeanings.name: Lord,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably a respelling of   Laura  , that coincides in form with the German spelling of the same name and also with the medieval English given name.

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn was born in 1552, in Lanark, South Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. He married Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn about 1576, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons. He died on 15 August 1623, in his hometown, at the age of 71, and was buried in Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé le 15 août 1623, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Cleghorn, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghornは1552年にイギリススコットランドソウス ラナルクシュアイアラナルクで生まれた。約1576年にイギリススコットランドでLady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghornと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子の親であった。彼は1623年8月15日に故郷で71歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアクレグホールンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn(은)는 1552년 영국 스코틀랜드 소우스 랜아르크실르 랜아르크에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1576년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1623년 8월 15일 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 클레고른에 묻혔다.

Lockhart of Cleghorn aproximadamente 1576, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Lord, Alan Lockhart VI of Ceghorn родился в 1552 г. в местности ланарк, соут ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady, Elizabeth, M. Lockhart of Cleghorn около 1576 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей. Он умер 15 августа 1623 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности клегаурн, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
